author: Gurdit Singh, Bhai
title: Qissa Yari Khuwari arthat Hir Ranjha, Mirza Sahiban, Shirin Farihad, Sohni Mahiwal adik prachin wa nawin ashkan da hu bahu naqsha
gloss: A brief account of the love affairs of the above named lovers, with the author's comments.
notes: Compendium of the classic romances (Hir-Ranjha, Mirza-Sahiban, Shirin-Farhad, Sohni-Mahiwal) with moral commentary.

author: Gurdit Singh, Bhai
title: Ath Sidak Bharosa Siri Guru Tegh Bhadur ji
gloss: Verses in eulogy of Guru Tegh Bahadur, one of the Gurus or spiritual leaders of the Sikhs.

author: Gurdit Singh, Sant
title: No. 1 charbainte Pashtu di sur ute prasang Siri Guru Nanak Sahib ji de avtar dharan ar mullan pas parhan ate janju pawan da
gloss: An account of the birth of Guru Nanak, the founder of the Sikh religion, and his strange talks with his teachers about the letters of the alphabet and the sacred thread worn by the Hindus.
notes: Guru Nanak janamsakhi episodes (schooling, refusal of the janeu) set to a Pashto tune ('charbainte Pashtu di sur ute') — a Sikh hagiography sung to a Pashtun melody, from Bannu on the frontier; music/tune-crossing data point.

author: Harnam Singh, Bhai, Ahluwalya
title: Nawan Qissa Hun de Jamane da hal
gloss: A poem satirising the want of love and sympathy exhibited by relatives of the present day.

author: Hukam Singh, Bhai
title: Baran Mah Gur Upkar
gloss: A brief account of the self-sacrifice of Guru Gobind Singh, the 10th Guru of the Sikhs.
notes: Baran-mah (twelve-months) form; author from a numbered canal-colony chak.

author: Ishar Singh, Bhai
title: Bujhakki Nama
gloss: Verses explaining the purpose of the incarnation of Guru Gobind Singh, the 10th Sikh Guru.

author: Kali Das, Lala
title: Rup Basant
gloss: Story noticed in entry No. 136 at page 24 of the catalogue for the quarter ending September, 1908.
notes: Registration No. 1 of the year 1910 — the first book registered. Rup Basant folk-romance, 4th edition. pencil note in left margin ('4th edition recd' or similar).
